data.title: Federal Reserve Highlights Increased Uncertainty in Economic Outlook
data.description: According to BlockBeats, the Federal Reserve reiterated its focus on the risks associated with its dual mandate in a recent FOMC statement. The statement emphasized that uncertainty regarding the economic outlook has "further increased." While the unemployment rate has stabilized and the labor market remains resilient, the risks of high unemployment and inflation have risen. Despite fluctuations in net exports affecting data, economic activity continues to expand at a "robust pace."

data.title: Traders Anticipate Federal Reserve Rate Cuts by July
data.description: According to BlockBeats, following the Federal Reserve's recent interest rate decision, traders continue to expect a rate cut by July. The market still anticipates three rate reductions within this year.

data.title: Federal Reserve Acknowledges Economic Challenges Amid GDP Decline
data.description: According to Odaily, Federal Reserve officials noted in their May policy statement that the GDP growth for the first quarter turned negative. Despite this, the Federal Reserve's policy committee still considers the underlying economy to be strong. They identified significant fluctuations in trade statistics as the primary cause for the GDP contraction in the first quarter, with imports surging ahead of new tariffs. The statement highlighted that, although net export volatility affected the data, recent indicators suggest that economic activity continues to expand at a robust pace. However, the new language in this month's policy statement also acknowledges that the risks of rising unemployment and inflation have intensified.

data.title: U.S. Dollar Index Experiences Brief Decline
data.description: According to Odaily, the U.S. Dollar Index (DXY) experienced a short-term decrease of 10 points, currently standing at 99.49.

data.title: The three major U.S. stock indexes remained stable, with the Dow Jones Industrial Average up 0.61%
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: The three major U.S. stock indexes remained stable, with the Dow Jones Industrial Average up 0.61%, the S&P 500 up 0.15%, and the Nasdaq down 0.29%. (Jinshi)

data.title: Federal Reserve FOMC: Risks of rising unemployment and inflation have increased
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Federal Reserve officials said in their May policy statement that GDP growth turned negative in the first quarter, but even so, the Fed's policy committee still believes that the underlying economy is strong. They observed that large fluctuations in trade statistics were the main reason why GDP fell into contraction in the first quarter, as imports surged before the new tariffs. "Although fluctuations in net exports have affected the data, recent indicators suggest that economic activity continues to expand at a solid pace," the statement read. However, in the new language of this month's policy statement, officials also acknowledged that "the risks of rising unemployment and inflation have intensified." (Jinshi)

data.title: Strive to become Bitcoin treasury company
data.description:
Strive Asset Management, founded by entrepreneur and former presidential candidate Vivek Ramaswamy, has revealed plans to transition into a Bitcoin treasury company. According to a May 7 announcement, Strive is going public through a reverse merger and plans to use the combined company’s stock to accumulate Bitcoin (BTC). The deal will see Strive merging with Asset Entities — a social media marketing company listed on the Nasdaq. The combined entity will operate under the Strive brand and use its access to the public equity markets to finance Bitcoin purchases, the company said
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/strive-to-become-bitcoin-treasury-company?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: Russia surges ahead in Bitcoin mining amid Trump-Era tariffs and U.S. cost pressures
data.description:
8-5-2025 – Russia has emerged as a primary beneficiary of Bitcoin mining revenues, propelled by new trade policies under U. S. President Donald Trump. The imposition of hefty tariffs—25% on imports from Canada and Mexico, and 10% on Chinese goods—has reshaped economic dynamics, inadvertently bolstering Russia’s position in the crypto-mining sector
source: https://www.cointrackdaily.com/russia-surges-ahead-in-bitcoin-mining-amid-trump-era-tariffs-and-u-s-cost-pressures/
